info.name: CDSS Platform API
info.description:
Clinical Decision Support System (CDSS) Platform – Complete API collection.

## Setup
1. Import this collection and the `CDSS_Platform.postman_environment.json` environment file.
2. Set `base_url` to your server (default: `http://localhost:8000`).
3. Run **POST /auth/token** (User Login) or **POST /auth/sessions** (System Login) first.
4. The `access_token` variable is auto-saved by the login requests – all protected requests use it automatically.

## Auth Flows
- **Human users** (clinicians, admins): use `POST /auth/token`
- **Machine-to-machine** (HMIS/EMR/PHR/DHA): use `POST /auth/sessions`

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description: Token management – login, refresh, logout, user info, and JWKS.

request.description:
Resource Owner Password Flow for human users (clinicians, nurses, admins).
Returns access_token + refresh_token, both auto-saved to collection variables.

response.name: 503 Keycloak Unavailable

request.description:
Machine-to-machine Client Credentials Flow for HMIS/EMR/PHR/DHA systems.
Send client_id + client_secret; CDSS proxies to Keycloak and returns RS256 signed JWT.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access token. New tokens are auto-saved to collection variables.
Revokes JTI in Redis and optionally calls Keycloak end-session. Requires a valid Bearer token.

response.name: 200 OK – Logged Out

request.description: Returns decoded identity claims from the validated JWT. No database lookup.
Proxy Keycloak JWKS so downstream systems (HMIS/EMR/PHR/DHA) can verify tokens without direct Keycloak access.

item.name: 🩺 Patients
item.description:
Patient registration and summary retrieval.
Required scopes: `cdss:patient:read`, `cdss:patient:write`, `cdss:encounter:read`, `cdss:recommendation:read`.

request.description:
Retrieve patient demographics, active diagnoses, allergies, and current medications.
Scope required: `cdss:patient:read`

Register a new patient record.
Scope required: `cdss:patient:write`

List all encounters for a patient.
Scope required: `cdss:encounter:read`

List all CDSS recommendations generated for a patient.
Scope required: `cdss:recommendation:read`

item.name: 🏥 Encounters
item.description:
Encounter management and clinical timeline.
Required scopes: `cdss:encounter:read`, `cdss:encounter:write`.

request.description:
Retrieve full encounter details including diagnoses and timeline events.
Scope required: `cdss:encounter:read`

response.name: 200 OK – Encounter Detail

request.description:
Open a new clinical encounter.
Scope required: `cdss:encounter:write`

Retrieve clinical timeline events for an encounter, sorted newest-first.
Scope required: `cdss:encounter:read`

response.name: 200 OK – Timeline

item.name: 💊 Medications
item.description:
Drug formulary, safety checks, interaction checks, and prescribing.
Required scopes: `cdss:medication:read`, `cdss:medication:write`.

request.description:
Check if a drug is safe for a patient given their eGFR and documented allergies.
Returns contraindications, warnings, and adjusted dose recommendations.
Scope required: `cdss:medication:read`

**Available drugs:** ticagrelor, clopidogrel, prasugrel, fondaparinux, enoxaparin, atorvastatin, metoprolol

response.name: 200 OK – Contraindicated (eGFR low)
response.body:
{
  "drug": "Prasugrel",
  "patient_id": "PAT-CARD-001",
  "safe_to_use": false,
  "warnings": [],
  "contraindications_triggered": ["Prasugrel contraindicated: eGFR 22 < 30"],
  "dose_recommendation": "60mg loading, then 10mg daily"
}

request.description:
Full formulary information for a drug: class, standard dose, renal adjustment, contraindications, interactions, monitoring.
Scope required: `cdss:medication:read`

**Try:** ticagrelor | clopidogrel | prasugrel | fondaparinux | enoxaparin | atorvastatin | metoprolol

response.name: 200 OK – Drug Info
response.body:
{
  "drug_name": "Ticagrelor",
  "drug_class": "P2Y12 inhibitor (antiplatelet)",
  "standard_dose": "180mg loading, then 90mg twice daily",
  "renal_adjustment": "No dose adjustment required for eGFR >= 15",
  "contraindications": [
    "active bleeding",
    "prior intracranial haemorrhage",
    "severe hepatic impairment"
  ],
  "interactions": [
    "strong CYP3A4 inhibitors",
    "digoxin",
    "simvastatin >40mg"
  ],
  "monitoring": ["dyspnoea", "bleeding signs", "renal function"],
  "notes": "Preferred P2Y12 inhibitor for NSTEMI. NOT affected by aspirin allergy."
}

request.description:
Check all pairwise drug-drug interactions for a list of drugs.
Pass an array of drug names. Returns severity + recommendation for each interaction found.
Scope required: `cdss:medication:read`

response.name: 200 OK – Interactions Found

request.description:
Record a prescription for a patient. Requires elevated write scope.
Scope required: `cdss:medication:write`

item.name: 🤖 Clinical Decision Support
item.description:
Core CDSS recommendation engine: generate AI recommendations and submit human review decisions.
This module runs the full 6-layer Clinical Intelligence Pipeline (RAG → Claude LLM → Safety Gate → Human Review).

body.raw:
{
  "patientId": "PAT-CARD-001",
  "encounterId": "ENC-CARD-001",
  "userId": "cardiologist@hospital.local",
  "userRole": "CARDIOLOGIST",
  "query": "What antiplatelet and anticoagulation therapy should be initiated for this NSTEMI patient with aspirin allergy and eGFR 42?",
  "consentVerified": true,
  "patientContext": {
    "age": 68,
    "sex": "male",
    "encounterType": "cardiology-consult",
    "diagnoses": ["NSTEMI", "type-2-diabetes", "chronic-kidney-disease"],
    "ecgFindings": ["ST depression in lateral leads V4-V6", "T-wave inversion"],
    "labs": {
      "troponin": "elevated 2.4 ng/mL",
      "eGFR": "42 mL/min/1.73m2",
      "potassium": "4.8 mEq/L",
      "creatinine": "1.8 mg/dL",
      "haemoglobin": "11.2 g/dL",
      "platelets": "210,000/μL",
      "INR": "1.1"
    },
    "vitals": {
      "systolicBp": "128 mmHg",
      "diastolicBp": "82 mmHg",
      "heartRate": "88 bpm",
      "spo2": "97%",
      "respiratoryRate": "16/min",
      "temperature": "37.1°C"
    },
    "currentMedications": ["atorvastatin", "metoprolol"],
    "allergies": ["aspirin"],
    "contraindications": [],
    "cardiacHistory": ["hypertension", "previous PCI 3 years ago"]
  }
}

request.description:
**Core CDSS endpoint.** Runs the full 6-layer Clinical Intelligence Pipeline:
1. **RAG Retrieval** – pulls relevant cardiology guideline chunks from ChromaDB
2. **LLM Inference** – Claude generates structured clinical recommendation
3. **Safety Gate** – validates against allergy/renal/haemodynamic constraints
4. **Human Review Queue** – sets status to `pending_review`

Returns structured recommendation requiring cardiologist approval.
The `correlation_id` is auto-saved for use in the review endpoint.

⚠️ Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` to be configured in `.env`

response.name: 200 OK – Recommendation Generated
response.body:
{
  "correlation_id": "3fa85f64-5717-4562-b3fc-2c963f66afa6",
  "patient_id": "PAT-CARD-001",
  "encounter_id": "ENC-CARD-001",
  "query_type": "clinical",
  "recommendation": {
    "recommendation_id": "rec-9a1b2c3d",
    "summary": "Initiate ticagrelor 180mg loading (preferred P2Y12 – safe in aspirin allergy), fondaparinux 2.5mg SC OD (eGFR 42 ≥ 20 threshold), high-intensity atorvastatin 80mg, optimise metoprolol for HR 50-70bpm. Early invasive strategy (coronary angiography within 72h).",
    "risk_stratification": "HIGH – GRACE score estimated >140. Elevated troponin, ST changes, CKD stage 3a, T2DM.",
    "antiplatelet_guidance": "Ticagrelor 180mg loading dose, then 90mg BD. Aspirin allergy documented – ticagrelor monotherapy or clopidogrel 600mg loading as alternative.",
    "invasive_strategy": "Early invasive (within 24-72h). Cardiology review for coronary angiography ± PCI. Renal protection protocol given eGFR 42.",
    "adjunct_therapy": "Atorvastatin 80mg OD. Metoprolol titrate to HR 50-70 bpm. Hold ACEi/ARB until haemodynamically stable.",
    "monitoring_plan": "Troponin series at 3h and 6h. Repeat eGFR 24h post-contrast. K+ monitoring daily (current 4.8). ECG monitoring continuous.",
    "human_review_note": "Aspirin allergy mandates ticagrelor-based DAPT modification. eGFR 42 excludes prasugrel. Fondaparinux preferred over LMWH for eGFR 42. Requires senior cardiologist approval before implementation.",
    "ai_path_used": "rag_llm",
    "rag_driven": true,
    "evidence_documents": [
      {
        "doc_id": "chunk-001",
        "source": "CARDIO-ACS-NSTEMI-2026.txt",
        "content_snippet": "For NSTEMI with aspirin allergy, ticagrelor is the preferred P2Y12 inhibitor...",
        "similarity_score": 0.94,
        "source_type": "guideline",
        "relevance_tag": "antiplatelet"
      }
    ],
    "safety_flags": {
      "allergy_conflict": true,
      "contraindication_detected": false,
      "renal_dose_adjustment_required": true,
      "high_bleeding_risk": false,
      "haemodynamic_instability": false,
      "requires_urgent_escalation": false,
      "flags": ["aspirin_allergy_documented", "ckd_stage_3a_renal_adjustment"]
    },
    "confidence_score": 0.87,
    "decision_status": "pending_review",
    "requires_human_review": true,
    "generated_at": "2026-05-10T12:30:00Z"
  },
  "pipeline_latency_ms": 1840.5,
  "audit_logged": true,
  "model_version": "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022",
  "disclaimer": "This output is AI-generated clinical decision support. It requires cardiologist review before any care action. Not a substitute for clinical judgment."
}

response.name: 503 API Key Not Configured
422 Consent Not Verified

request.description:
Retrieve a previously generated recommendation by its correlation ID.
The `correlation_id` is auto-set when you run 'Generate CDSS Recommendation'.
List recent recommendations with summary status. Useful for dashboard/queue views.

body.raw:
{
  "correlation_id": "{{correlation_id}}",
  "reviewer_id": "dr.smith@hospital.local",
  "reviewer_role": "CARDIOLOGIST",
  "action": "approve",
  "notes": "Recommendation reviewed and approved. Ticagrelor appropriate given aspirin allergy. eGFR 42 acceptable for fondaparinux. Proceed with early invasive strategy.",
  "edited_summary": null
}

request.description:
**Layer 5 – Human Review.** Cardiologist submits approve/reject/edit decision.
Logs immutably per HIPAA audit requirements.

**Actions:** `approve` | `reject` | `edit`

The `correlation_id` is auto-filled from the Generate Recommendation response.

response.name: 400 Invalid Action

item.name: 📋 Audit
item.description:
HIPAA-compliant audit log access.
Required scope: `cdss:audit:read`.

request.description:
List recent audit events, newest first.
Scope required: `cdss:audit:read`

item.name: Get Audit Events by Correlation ID

request.description:
Retrieve all audit events for a specific correlation ID (traces a single recommendation lifecycle).
Scope required: `cdss:audit:read`

Export the complete audit log as JSONL (newline-delimited JSON).
Response is a file download (`cdss_audit.jsonl`).
Scope required: `cdss:audit:read`

item.name: ⚙️ Admin
item.description:
System administration – stats, audit queries, and RAG index management.
Required scope: `cdss:admin` (or `cdss:audit:read` for audit endpoints).

request.description:
Returns system health stats: app version, LLM model, RAG chunk count, audit log size, pending recommendation count.
Scope required: admin role

Admin audit query with filtering by event_type and patient_id.
Scope required: `cdss:audit:read`
Returns all unique event type strings present in the audit log. Useful for building filter dropdowns.

Re-ingest all guideline files from `./data/guidelines/` into ChromaDB.
Run this after adding or updating clinical guideline documents.
Scope required: admin role

⚠️ **Destructive.** Deletes the ChromaDB collection entirely.
Run `POST /admin/rag/reindex` afterwards to rebuild.
Scope required: admin role

item.name: 🏓 System Health
item.description: Health and readiness checks – no auth required.

request.description: System health check. Returns `healthy` if ChromaDB is ready, `degraded` otherwise. No auth required.
Detailed ChromaDB / RAG collection status: chunk count, embedding model, ready flag.
